g.text: Two-Phase Lock-Free Delete with Hazard Pointer Protection

div.p: Mark the node's next pointer Set low bit to indicate deletion. Other threads skip marked nodes. Atomic operation - no race window.
Invariant: Once marked, a node is never unmarked. Marked nodes are skipped in all traversals.
Unlink node from list Must use hazard pointers to protect both prev and curr during unlink. CAS prev->next to skip marked node.

ol.li: Snapshot ALL hazard pointers from ALL threads
Check each retired node against snapshot
Free only nodes NOT in snapshot

div.p: 1. Mark Before Unlink A node MUST be marked before physical removal. This prevents lost updates during concurrent inserts. 2. Hazard Protect Before Access MUST publish hazard pointer BEFORE dereferencing. Use set-then-validate protocol. 3. Never Free In-Use Nodes A node is only freed if:

ul.li: AND not in any hazard pointer slot 4. Read Value Before Unlink Read curr->value BEFORE the unlink CAS. After unlink, another thread may retire/free.

div.p: DANGER: Use-After-Free Race Without hazard pointers, this sequence causes use-after-free:
Fix: Thread A protects curr with hazard pointer BEFORE the unlink CAS. Thread B's scan sees the hazard pointer and defers free.

text.tspan: // Atomic CAS: mark if not already marked
// Atomic CAS: mark if not already marked
// Protect prev and curr with hazard ptrs
// Protect prev and curr with hazard ptrs

g.text: Only free if NO thread has HP to B
